mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values compress real equity valuations by increasing discount rates and eroding corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.5% quarterly signals capacity utilization tightening that forces central banks to preemptively tighten policy, causing negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive returns in >60% of late-cycle observations, or regime classification fails to improve out-of-sample Sharpe ratio by >0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in <50% of high-payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 2.0% indicates restrictive monetary conditions that suppress corporate borrowing and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered periods, or the threshold fails to generate a statistically significant return differential (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals corporate profit compression from inventory buildup, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term yields positive returns in >60% of cases, or model R-squared drops below 0.05 when interaction is included versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction during periods of low unemployment indicates supply-side bottlenecks that force immediate equity repricing, generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered regime observations, or regime filter reduces out-of-sample hit rate below baseline.
